mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 60% during co-declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and stronger economic output, which supports higher corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 60-day returns during recovery phases, with a negative return frequency above 50%, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a strengthening labor market and rising household income, which boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the signal yields negative returns in over 55% of expansionary months across a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 205.0, elevated inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive monetary tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods when CPIAUCSL surpasses 205.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling real personal consumption expenditures signals weak consumer-driven growth, which undermines equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with positive returns occurring in over 55% of divergence episodes, or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: High federal funds rates combined with rising unemployment indicate a policy-induced economic slowdown, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of high rates and rising unemployment fails to predict negative returns, with positive 20-day returns exceeding 50% of triggered instances, or the signal loses significance during low-inflation regimes.
